conversations.say: Arden, let's look at those Veridian archive records side by side with my letter packet. If there's an expense entry contradicting my memory, let's inspect it openly rather than hiding behind PR.
conversations.offer: Viewing of the First R Letter packet and editorial records.
conversations.request: Full access to Veridian's intake report and expense entries.

conversations.say: Edi, I've heard rumors about your commissioned performance. Let's talk frankly about what you were asked to enact so I can separate theatrical staging from my genuine early encounters.
conversations.offer: My perspective on early R reporting and newsroom traditions.
conversations.request: Clarification on what was commissioned for your performance and who stood behind it.

conversations.say: Rafi, you've kept records of shop habits and recurring routines. Let's compare what you observed across the bodega counter with what I saw in early R materials.
conversations.offer: My comparison between early R material and neighborhood habits.
conversations.request: Insight into how R's private habits persisted across years at your shop.

public_statement: Fifteen years of R is long enough to stop defending a pristine myth. Let's look at the actual intake reports, expense logs, and performance records without dodging where our memories were polished.

commitments.promise: I will share the First R Letter packet for inspection if Arden matches it with Veridian's intake report and expense records.

goal_progress: Engaged Arden Bell to review archive records and expense entries alongside the First R Letter packet.
Approached Edi Velez to discuss the boundaries of commissioned performances versus genuine encounters.
Consulted Rafi Calderón to compare shop routines and recurring private habits.
reasoning_summary: In Section 2, Frankie Lowell confronts the discrepancies in the archive by collaborating with Arden Bell on Veridian intake and expense records, questioning Edi Velez about theatrical performance versus authorship, and comparing recurring habits with Rafi Calderón. Frankie maintains a balanced view while critically examining the legends.
